name: Page Tree
description: Hierarchical page navigation with drag-and-drop reordering, inline rename, and nested page support. Used in workspace sidebars.

standard.description: Full tree with icons, expand/collapse, and context menus

compact.description: Dense tree with smaller text and tighter row spacing for narrow sidebar containers

visual_brief: Sidebar navigation tree with nested page items. Each item shows a document icon, page title, and an expand/collapse chevron for nested children. Indentation increases per nesting level with a thin vertical guide line. Hovering a page reveals action icons (three-dot menu for rename, duplicate, delete). A 'Favorites' section at the top shows pinned pages with a star icon. A search input at the very top filters pages by name. A 'New Page' button at the bottom (or a plus icon at the top) creates new pages. The compact preset reduces row height and font size. The flat preset removes nesting and shows all pages in a flat alphabetical list.

layout_hints.tree_hierarchy: Nested structure should remain obvious through indentation, guide lines, and expand/collapse affordances. Do not flatten the mental model just to save horizontal space.
layout_hints.action_reveal: Row actions should feel contextual and low-noise. They may reveal on hover or focus, but must remain reachable for keyboard users.
layout_hints.editor_context: The tree is a navigation and structure surface, not a content card list. Keep row density operational and calm.

responsive.mobile: Page tree renders as a full-screen drawer or sheet. Larger touch targets for tree items. Drag-to-reorder uses long-press. Actions in swipe menus.
responsive.tablet: Sidebar at 260px width. Standard row heights. Drag-to-reorder functional.
responsive.desktop: Sidebar at 280px width with comfortable spacing. Full keyboard navigation. Drag-and-drop reordering.

motion.micro: Expand/collapse chevrons, hover states, and pinned/favorite toggles may animate subtly over 120-180ms.
motion.transitions: Nested branch expansion, drag-reorder previews, and drawer transitions should feel smooth and structured rather than flashy.

keyboard: Arrow keys expand, collapse, and move between tree items.
announcements: Expanded and collapsed branch states should be announced clearly.
Pinned or favorite state changes should be announced semantically.
accessibility.focus_management: When a branch expands or collapses, keep focus on the controlling tree item rather than jumping to a different depth unexpectedly.

composition.TreeItem: Row(hoverable, indent: level) > [ExpandChevron? + DocIcon + PageTitle(ellipsis) + ActionsMenu?(d-interactive, show-on-hover)]
